mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer demand and economic activity, which directly supports corporate revenues and earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending is a major component of GDP
failure_condition: If RSAFS growth is positive but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of observations over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and sustained increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a weakening labor market, which typically precedes or accompanies an economic downturn, negatively impacting corporate profits and equity valuations.
failure_condition: If, after a trigger, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of instances during periods of rising unemployment.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) is elevated and the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) is high and actively rising, it indicates an aggressive monetary tightening cycle designed to cool the economy, which typically leads to lower equity valuations.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL > 3% YoY AND FEDFUNDS rises > 0.25pp in 1 month
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances during periods of high inflation and rising FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: A strong rebound in housing starts (HOUST) during an economic recovery phase indicates renewed investment and consumer confidence in the housing sector, providing a positive short-term boost to market sentiment and S&P 500 returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing is a cyclical economic indicator
failure_condition: If, following a HOUST trigger in a recovery, S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals a contraction in the manufacturing sector and overall supply-side weakness, which negatively impacts corporate earnings and S&P 500 returns over a medium-term horizon.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 45% of instances following a significant INDPRO decline.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects a healthy and expanding labor market, supporting consumer income and spending, which generally leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns due to increased corporate revenues.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS growth is positive but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of observations over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant reduction in the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) signals an aggressive easing stance by the central bank, which is typically interpreted as supportive for equity markets by lowering borrowing costs and stimulating economic activity, leading to short-term positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: If, after a FEDFUNDS rate cut trigger, S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth (GDPC1) is strong and retail sales (RSAFS) are also accelerating, it indicates robust aggregate demand across the economy, providing a strong fundamental tailwind for corporate profits and S&P 500 performance over the medium term.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth > 2% YoY AND RSAFS growth > 0.5% MoM
evidence_refs: fact:GDP and retail sales reflect economic health
failure_condition: If, during periods of strong GDPC1 and accelerating RSAFS, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 35% of observations.
